admin.lang.label: Admin Language
admin.warning.default_password: You are still using the default password. Change it soon to keep the site safe.
admin.confirm.title: Confirm Delete
admin.common.name: Name
admin.common.title: Title
admin.dashboard.title: Overview
admin.dashboard.subtitle: Current content language: %s
admin.dashboard.todo_desc: Prioritize drafts, scheduled publishing, and missing content fields.
admin.dashboard.todo_empty: No obvious action items right now
admin.dashboard.todo_empty_desc: Create more content, or review recent updates.
admin.dashboard.system_status_desc: Security, version, and external access status.
admin.dashboard.recent_desc: Recently edited posts, links, and pages.
admin.dashboard.automation_desc: What AI assistants and external tools have done recently.
admin.dashboard.automation_empty_desc: After creating access, AI activity will appear here.
admin.dashboard.task.post_drafts_hint: Waiting for review, completion, or publishing
admin.dashboard.task.post_scheduled_hint: Confirm publishing time and content status
admin.dashboard.task.link_drafts_hint: Confirm target URLs and detail pages
admin.dashboard.task.link_scheduled_hint: Confirm publishing time and target URL
admin.dashboard.task.missing_cover_hint: Affects lists, sharing, and AI review quality
admin.dashboard.task.missing_category_hi: Affects category pages and content filtering
admin.dashboard.status.api_empty_hint: Create access before AI can connect safely
admin.dashboard.status.version_hint: Check for new releases in System Updates
admin.dashboard.status.password_ok_hint: The admin password is not the default value
admin.sites.title: Site Management
admin.sites.subtitle: Manage platform sites, domains, the default entry, and platform automation access.
admin.sites.current_desc: Review each site's status, bound domains, storage paths, and platform entry.
admin.sites.create_desc: A new site gets its own cms.db and uploads directory. The domain can be added later.
admin.sites.platform_api_enable_new: Allow automation through the platform entry
admin.sites.name: Site
admin.posts.title: Posts
admin.posts.empty: No posts in this language yet. Create one from the top right.
admin.links.title: Links
admin.links.count: %d links (resources / product showcases with detail pages and target URLs)
admin.links.empty: No links in this language yet. Create one from the top right.
admin.pages.title: Pages
admin.pages.desc: Standalone pages such as About are managed here.
admin.pages.empty: No pages in this language yet.
admin.login.title: Admin Login
admin.login.too_many: Too many login attempts. Try again in about %d minutes.
admin.edit.link_url: Link URL (target URL; the front-end Visit button opens it)
admin.edit.slug_placeholder: Leave blank to generate from title
admin.edit.author: Author
admin.edit.author_placeholder: Leave empty to use default author: %s
admin.edit.remove_cover_confirm: Remove this cover image? Save to apply.
admin.edit.rich_placeholder: Start writing… Select text for tools; use an empty line plus + to insert blocks.
admin.edit.comments_note: Enable Giscus under Settings → Comments first.
admin.edit.meta_desc: Custom Meta Description (uses excerpt when empty)
admin.edit.translations_desc: Each language version is maintained independently and linked for front-end language switching and hreflang.
admin.edit.heading: Heading
admin.edit.link_pop_placeholder: Paste a link and press Enter
admin.visual.note: In edit mode, click editable content in the preview; in browse mode, you can navigate inside the preview.
admin.visual.pick_desc: Click the site name, hero copy, section titles, footer copy or navigation labels in the preview.
admin.visual.hint.site_name: Shown in the header, footer and SEO site name.
admin.visual.hint.tagline: Used in browser titles and secondary copy in some themes.
admin.visual.hint.description: Homepage hero description and the default SEO description.
admin.visual.hint.logo: Shown in the header and footer; empty uses the built-in default logo.
admin.visual.hint.favicon: Shown in browser tabs. SVG, PNG or ICO is recommended.
admin.visual.hint.share_image: Default image for sharing to WeChat, Feishu, X and similar platforms. Recommended 1200×630.
admin.visual.hint.hero_eyebrow: Small copy above the homepage main title.
admin.visual.hint.hero_title: The most prominent title on the homepage. Keep it short.
admin.visual.hint.hero_image: Uploading switches the homepage right-side visual to image mode.
admin.visual.hint.footer_note: Shown below the site name in the footer.
admin.visual.hint.nav: Only changes the current language label. The path is still managed under Navigation settings.
admin.visual.hint.about_title: Main title of the About page.
admin.visual.hint.about_excerpt: Short description under the About page title.
admin.visual.hint.about_content: Supports Markdown; edit long content from Pages when needed.
admin.visual.hint.category_title: Title of the all-posts category list page.
admin.visual.hint.category_description: Shown below the all-posts category page title.
admin.visual.hint.category_all_button: Display text for the All button in post category navigation.
admin.visual.hint.category_nav_name: Category navigation button text; does not change the URL.
admin.visual.hint.category_item_descript: Shown below the current category page title.
admin.visual.hint.links_title: Title at the top of the links list page.
admin.visual.hint.links_description: Shown below the links list page title.
admin.visual.hint.links_all_button: Display text for the All button in link category navigation.
admin.visual.hint.link_category_nav_name: Link category navigation button text; does not change the URL.
admin.visual.hint.link_category_item_des: Shown below the current link category page title.
admin.settings.title: Site Settings
admin.settings.cloudflare.title: Cloudflare Deploy
admin.settings.cloudflare.desc: The frontend is exported as a static site; the admin and database stay on this server; content changes can sync to Cloudflare automatically.
admin.settings.cloudflare.desc_static: The frontend is exported as a static site.
admin.settings.cloudflare.desc_backend: The admin and database stay on this server.
admin.settings.cloudflare.desc_sync: Content changes can sync to Cloudflare automatically.
admin.settings.cloudflare.token_note: Use the template above to create a dedicated Cloudflare API Token. It includes Worker Assets, Pages, DNS, cache purge, Zone Read, and Account Read in one pass. The token is stored only in the local database and is never shown again.
admin.settings.cloudflare.token_keep: Already saved. Leave empty to keep it.
admin.settings.cloudflare.connect_desc: Enter the public domain, open the authorization template, paste the Token here, then save and deploy.
admin.settings.cloudflare.bound_desc: Configuration is saved. Publish content updates to Cloudflare below, or modify binding when you change the domain, Token, or project name.
admin.settings.cloudflare.step_zone_read: Confirm the domain is on Cloudflare
admin.settings.cloudflare.local_note: Yes. You can export and upload the static site locally. After deployment, public visits are served from Cloudflare static assets and do not require Cloudflare to reach your local machine.
admin.settings.cloudflare.template_step_: In Cloudflare, choose the account and domain scope that owns the public site domain.
Continue to summary and confirm Worker scripts, Worker routes, Pages, DNS, cache purge, Zone Read, and Account Read are included.
Create the Token, copy the one-time value, paste it here, then save.
admin.settings.cloudflare.public_domain_: The domain visitors will open. If it is hosted in the authorized Cloudflare account, gcms can bind it automatically; otherwise configure DNS manually.
admin.settings.cloudflare.alias_placehol: For example www.ccvar.com, one per line
admin.settings.cloudflare.redirect_alias: 301 redirect aliases to the primary domain
admin.settings.cloudflare.alias_hint: Alias domains will redirect or serve the same site based on this setting.
admin.settings.cloudflare.single_domain_: Only one public domain is currently bound.
admin.settings.cloudflare.zone_hint_subd: The entry %s you entered usually belongs to the %s Cloudflare Zone. Choose that Zone when creating the Token.
admin.settings.cloudflare.zone_hint_root: The entry you entered maps to this Cloudflare Zone: %s.
admin.settings.cloudflare.dns_hint: Deployment will confirm Cloudflare DNS for %s and create the right proxied record for the current hosting mode when none exists.
admin.settings.cloudflare.template_hint: Cloudflare shows the Token only once. Copy it from the success page and paste it here.
admin.settings.cloudflare.saved_desc: The configuration has been verified and saved. You can deploy the static site now, or deploy later by clicking “Deploy now” in Deploy Status below.
admin.settings.cloudflare.token_hidden: The full Token is never shown again. Paste a new one if you need to replace it.
admin.settings.cloudflare.project_hint: Defaults to the primary domain. Click the edit icon to customize it.
admin.settings.cloudflare.reset_confirm: Only clear the Cloudflare Token, domains, and detected IDs saved locally. This will not delete projects, DNS records, or static assets in Cloudflare.
admin.settings.cloudflare.mode_worker_de: Stronger entry control for long-running hosting, route protection, and edge access rules.
admin.settings.cloudflare.mode_worker_no: Appears as a Worker in Cloudflare; file list is not shown
admin.settings.cloudflare.mode_pages_des: Feels like a static-site project, with project view, deployment history, and custom domains in Cloudflare.
admin.settings.cloudflare.mode_pages_not: Good for content sites and visual deployment history
admin.settings.cloudflare.zone_placehold: Zone ID for the domain; optional but recommended
admin.settings.cloudflare.zone_manual_hi: If detection still fails, copy the Zone ID from the %s domain overview in Cloudflare and paste it below. %s is the public site domain, not the Zone name.
If detection still fails, copy the Zone ID from the %s domain overview in Cloudflare and paste it below.
admin.settings.cloudflare.route_hint: For example example.com or www.example.com. gcms converts it to the Cloudflare route format internally.
admin.settings.cloudflare.cache_hint: The static site is hosted by Cloudflare; content changes take effect by republishing.
admin.settings.cloudflare.auto_sync: Republish the static site after content or config changes
admin.settings.cloudflare.sync_desc: By default, saved content syncs automatically. Use daily sync when you want changes published together, or manual sync when you want to deploy yourself.
admin.settings.cloudflare.sync_pending: Content changes are waiting to sync
admin.settings.cloudflare.sync_daily_not: When something changes, publish once at the selected daily time.
admin.settings.cloudflare.sync_realtime_: After content is saved, republish to Cloudflare automatically.
admin.settings.cloudflare.sync_manual_no: Content changes will not publish automatically. Deploy manually when you want to sync to Cloudflare.
admin.settings.cloudflare.sync_manual_pe: Content changes are waiting for manual sync.
admin.settings.cloudflare.source_fronten: After deployment, this server still serves the admin. The origin frontend defaults to a 301 redirect to the Cloudflare primary domain to avoid duplicate SEO content.
admin.settings.cloudflare.not_configured: Create and paste a Token first
admin.settings.cloudflare.route_managed: Domain is in the current Cloudflare Zone and DNS can be taken over automatically
admin.settings.cloudflare.route_manual: Deployment will try to detect Zone ID automatically
admin.settings.cloudflare.deploy_confirm: Export the current frontend as a static site and deploy it to Cloudflare? Public visits will be served by Cloudflare static assets after deployment.
admin.settings.cloudflare.purge_confirm: Clear the Cloudflare cache for this Zone? Static assets remain deployed and the next visit will read the deployed files again.
admin.settings.cloudflare.unpublish_conf: Remove public access for the current Cloudflare domain? The project and static assets stay in Cloudflare, and DNS records are not deleted.
admin.settings.cloudflare.unpublish_tip: Remove the public entry binding for the current domain in Cloudflare. The project and uploaded static assets stay, and DNS records are not deleted.
admin.settings.cloudflare.purge_tip: Clear Cloudflare edge cache. This does not delete the static site; the next visit will read the latest deployed assets again.
admin.settings.cloudflare.flow_1: gcms first generates complete static files from the current content, languages, theme, and public domain.
admin.settings.cloudflare.flow_2: The HTML, images, CSS, JS, and search indexes are uploaded to the selected Cloudflare hosting mode: Worker Assets or Pages.
admin.settings.cloudflare.flow_3: Public visitors only hit the pure static site on Cloudflare; admin pages, automation APIs, and preview links stay on the current gcms server.
admin.settings.cloudflare.flow_4: When content is saved, auto sync exports and republishes the static site after a short delay.
admin.settings.menu.lang_note: Navigation labels are edited per language in each row; paths are shared site-wide.
admin.settings.config.editing_suffix: front-end copy. Empty fields fall back to the default language.
admin.settings.visual.title: Visual Edit
admin.settings.visual.desc: Click content on the real site, preview changes live, and save settings more intuitively.
admin.settings.visual.preview_desc: A lightweight Go and SQLite content site focused on backend engineering, minimal design, and search optimization.
admin.settings.site.name: Site Name
admin.settings.site.default_author_hint: Used when a post author is left empty.
admin.settings.site.default_link_author_: Used when a link author is left empty.
admin.settings.site.remove_favicon_confi: Remove the current favicon? Save site info to apply.
admin.settings.site.remove_logo_confirm: Remove the current site logo? Save site info to apply.
admin.settings.site.remove_share_confirm: Remove the current share image? Save site info to apply.
admin.settings.site.share_note: Used as the default image when shared to WeChat, Feishu, X, and similar platforms. Post covers take priority. Recommended size: 1200 × 630.
admin.settings.site.social_note: Add homepage or profile URLs. Icons are detected from domains such as GitHub, X, YouTube, Telegram, LinkedIn, Weibo, and mailto:. Empty labels are named automatically; empty URLs are hidden.
admin.settings.site.delete_social_confir: Delete this social link? Save site info to apply.
admin.settings.site.home_counts_note: Site-wide content density settings. Set links to 0 to hide the Links section on the homepage.
admin.settings.site.code_note: Site-wide. Use this for trusted analytics, site verification, or advertising snippets. The code is output as-is on every public page.
admin.settings.site.inject_body_placehol: <!-- Example: ads, support widget, or analytics <script> -->
admin.settings.appearance.tweak: Tweak the selected theme accent color and radius. Each theme keeps its own tweaks.
admin.settings.appearance.hero_visual_de: The default is a tech-style animation. You can replace it with an image, SVG file, or pasted SVG code.
admin.settings.appearance.remove_hero_co: Remove the current homepage visual image? Save appearance to apply.
admin.settings.appearance.themes_desc: The 19 themes use different structures for headers, homepage lists, and cards. Select a theme, then save above to apply it.
admin.settings.copy.intro_suffix: ” front-end copy. Empty fields fall back to the default language.
admin.settings.copy.hero_image_global_no: The default language image is used as the site-wide Hero image. Other languages inherit it unless they set their own image.
admin.settings.copy.hero_image_lang_note: Chinese and English pages can use different Hero images. When unset, this language inherits the site-wide default image.
admin.settings.copy.remove_hero_image_co: Remove the current Hero image? Save copy to apply.
admin.settings.copy.home_titles_note: H2 titles for homepage sections. Empty fields use that language's default value, shown as gray placeholder text.
admin.settings.menu.preview_note: Updates live from the current order and labels
admin.settings.menu.desc: Choose common destinations to auto-fill paths and language labels. Use Custom internal path for special pages, and External link for other websites. Drag the handle to reorder.
admin.settings.menu.delete_confirm: Delete this menu item? Save the menu to apply.
admin.settings.categories.desc_post: Posts can select categories on the edit page. Post and link categories are independent. Drag rows to control the public category navigation order.
admin.settings.categories.desc_link: Links can select categories on the edit page. Post and link categories are independent. Drag rows to control the public category navigation order.
admin.settings.categories.post_all_desc: The default entry is /category. After setting a custom slug, /category/{slug} can also show all posts.
admin.settings.categories.link_all_desc: The default entry is /links. After setting a custom slug, /links/{slug} can also show all links.
admin.settings.categories.empty: No categories in this language yet. Create one from the top right.
admin.settings.categories.delete_confirm: Delete category “%s”? Content in this category will become uncategorized.
admin.settings.automation.posts_desc: Read posts, preview drafts, create drafts, and edit drafts. If a specific post needs changes, the assistant should first find its ID by title or slug.
admin.settings.automation.links_desc: Read and maintain links, preview link drafts, and manage target URLs, descriptions, content, and SEO copy.
admin.settings.automation.pages_desc: Read and maintain pages such as About pages, landing pages, and their SEO copy.
admin.settings.automation.docs_desc: Parameters, request examples, and common usage are documented here.
admin.settings.automation.note: Create one access key per external tool or platform. If you stop using it or suspect a leak, revoke that specific key.
admin.settings.automation.secret_once: The full key is shown only after creation or regeneration. When newly generated, use “View” on the matching row. After a refresh, regenerate it if you need the full key again.
admin.settings.automation.edit_permissio: Only the purpose name and allowed content operations change. The copied key itself stays the same.
admin.settings.automation.empty: No external assistant access has been created yet. Click “Create Access” first; after creation, use “View” in the list to copy the key.
admin.settings.automation.secret_note: The full key is shown only once. Download the AI integration kit; it includes the key file, OpenAPI description, and instructions for AI assistants.
admin.settings.automation.integration_no: OpenAPI describes the interface and is shared by all access keys. The access key above is what actually authorizes site operations. After creation, download the dedicated kit that already includes the key.
admin.settings.automation.help_1: Create a separate access key for each external tool, then copy the full key immediately.
admin.settings.automation.help_2: For tools like Codex, Claude Code, or Cursor, download the dedicated integration kit after creation.
admin.settings.automation.help_3: For ChatGPT Actions, Copilot Studio, or developer tools, copy the OpenAPI URL and configure the interface and key in that platform.
admin.settings.automation.help_note: Do not paste real keys into ordinary chat windows. Let AI save drafts by default, then review in the admin before publishing.
admin.settings.automation.openapi_desc: For ChatGPT Actions, Copilot Studio, or developer tools to understand the API.
admin.settings.automation.languages_read: List the default and enabled languages
admin.settings.automation.media_upload_d: Upload images and return URLs usable as covers
admin.settings.automation.posts_read_des: List, view, and preview post drafts
admin.settings.automation.posts_categori: List post categories and available IDs
admin.settings.automation.posts_write_de: Create drafts and edit unpublished posts
admin.settings.automation.posts_publish_: Publish, schedule, and edit published posts
admin.settings.automation.links_read_des: List, view, and preview link drafts
admin.settings.automation.links_categori: List link categories and available IDs
admin.settings.automation.links_write_de: Create drafts and edit unpublished links
admin.settings.automation.links_publish_: Publish, schedule, and edit published links
admin.settings.automation.pages_write_de: Create drafts and edit unpublished pages
admin.settings.automation.pages_publish_: Publish, schedule, and edit published pages
admin.settings.automation.regenerate_con: Regenerate the access key for “%s”? The old key will stop working immediately, and external tools using it must switch to the new key.
admin.settings.automation.revoke_confirm: Revoke access for “%s”? Automated jobs using this key will stop immediately.
admin.settings.automation.delete_confirm: Delete the revoked access record “%s”? It will no longer appear in the list.
admin.settings.comments.desc: Comments are not stored in GCMS. When enabled, post pages load giscus and host comments in your selected GitHub Discussions.
admin.settings.comments.prepare_1: Prepare a GitHub repository, enable Discussions, and install the giscus App.
admin.settings.comments.prepare_2_suffix: , then select the repository and discussion category.
admin.settings.comments.prepare_3: Paste the generated script into Quick Setup; GCMS will extract the Repo ID and Category ID automatically.
admin.settings.comments.prepare_4: Enable “Show comments” on the posts that need a comment section.
admin.settings.comments.prepare_note: Follow the 5 steps below. You do not need to understand Repo ID or Category ID manually; GCMS extracts the required values from the generated giscus script.
admin.settings.comments.quick_desc: Complete the 5 steps in order. When all four items in Current Config have values, save and enable it at the bottom.
admin.settings.comments.ready_hint: The configuration is complete and can be saved.
admin.settings.comments.pending_hint: After step 4 is parsed, the four configuration values will appear here.
admin.settings.comments.repo_hint: Use the owner/repo part of the GitHub repository URL, for example yourname/blog-comments.
admin.settings.comments.script_placehold: Paste the <script ...></script> generated by giscus.app here
admin.settings.comments.script_hint: Used only for this save action and not stored in the database.
admin.settings.comments.parse_ok: Configuration detected from the script. You can save and enable it.
admin.settings.comments.parse_missing: Could not detect a complete giscus config. Make sure the pasted script includes data-repo-id and data-category-id.
admin.settings.comments.step_repo_desc: Create or choose a public GitHub repository for comment discussions, and enable Discussions on that repository.
admin.settings.comments.step_app_desc: Click install, choose the repository from step 1, then return to this page.
admin.settings.comments.step_config_desc: Open the configuration page, use the same owner/repo, choose General or Announcements, and copy the script generated near the bottom.
admin.settings.comments.step_paste_desc: Paste the full generated script below, then click “Parse and fill”. GCMS will fill the advanced ID fields automatically.
admin.settings.comments.step_save_desc: After the four values on the right are filled, click “Save and Enable Giscus” below. Then enable “Show comments” on individual post edit pages.
admin.settings.comments.config_note: These values are generated by the giscus configuration page. GCMS only places them on post pages and does not store comments.
admin.settings.security.desc: Change the admin login password. The new password must be at least 6 characters.
admin.settings.security.demo_warning: These actions are for testing or new-site initialization. They will not delete the admin account, password, logo, favicon, uploads, or current theme, but they do affect the current content area. Make sure you have a backup.
admin.settings.security.reload_demo_conf: This will replace the current posts, pages, links, categories, navigation, and homepage copy with the latest gcms product-site demo. This cannot be undone. Continue?
admin.settings.security.clear_demo_confi: Clear built-in demo posts, pages, links, categories, and sample navigation? This cannot be undone.
admin.settings.updates.unsupported: This directory does not support in-admin upgrades
admin.settings.updates.login_expired: Your login expired. Log in again to view the upgrade result.
admin.settings.updates.wait_restart: The service is restarting and will recover shortly...
admin.settings.updates.csrf_expired: This page token has expired. Refresh System Updates and try Upgrade Now again.
admin.settings.updates.request_sent: Request sent. Waiting for service response...
admin.settings.updates.task_started: Upgrade task started. Waiting for the upgrader to take over.
admin.settings.updates.unreachable: Update source timed out or is unreachable
admin.settings.updates.no_asset: This release does not include a package for the current platform.
admin.settings.languages.front_desc: Choose languages enabled on the public site. The default language is the fallback when a visitor's browser language does not match an enabled language; it is also the fallback for copy and content. / negotiates by browser language first, and each language uses URLs such as /en/….
admin.settings.languages.custom_desc: Need another language? Add any locale here: code is used as the URL prefix, such as /pt/…, and tag is the BCP47 tag, such as pt-BR. Enable it above after adding. Custom front-end UI text falls back to the default language.
admin.settings.languages.delete_confirm: Delete custom language “%s”? Content in that language will not be deleted, but the public URL will no longer be reachable.
admin.settings.admin_i18n.title: Admin Interface Translation
admin.settings.admin_i18n.desc: The admin reads built-in JSON by default. Override keys for the current admin language here; missing keys or missing JSON fall back to Chinese.
admin.settings.admin_i18n.customize_desc: Advanced, low-frequency setting. Expand only when you need to override built-in translation keys.
